findings.description: Source unit contains a second directive ('Confirm audience in every brief before writing') that is a process/governance requirement distinct from the language-substitution rule. It is not represented anywhere in the candidate rules, so this compliance step is dropped entirely.

payload.description: Confirm target audience (patient/DTC vs HCP) in every creative brief before writing begins.

findings.description: Hardness classified as strong_default, but source uses an absolute, unqualified statement ('master reference ... across all LISRAYA materials') with no exception language, which reads as a hard_constraint rather than a default that can be overridden.

Scope conflict: u_0001 states the guide governs patient (DTC) materials specifically, while u_0002 asserts the patient brochure is master reference across 'all' LISRAYA materials (implying HCP materials too). This scope tension is not flagged or reconciled in either candidate rule's scope field.
